name: WSDOT - Active Transportation Level of Traffic Stress (LTS)
description: WSDOT evaluated and assigned a Level of Traffic Stress (LTS) ranking to WA state highway segments to aid in efforts to analyze high-level systemic safety and user acceptance of state highways for active transportation users. This data was also intended to inform high-level prioritization of active transportation improvements. LTS data rank highway segments from 1 to 4 based on roadway characteristics like posted speed, vehicle volume, number of travel lanes, and presence of active transportation

name: WSDOT - Active Transportation Route Directness Index Routes
description: This feature class represents the routes that were used for the Route Directness Index calculation. These routes were generated by submitting the begin and end points of each RDI_Transect to the Esri routing service using the Walking Distance parameter. The service returns a route of the shortest pedestrian path between the two points. name: WSDOT - Project Delivery Plan Current
provider: WSDOT CPDM Systems Support and Reporting, WSDOT CPDM, Transportation Planning Specialist
description: The 2024 Project Delivery Plan (“Plan”) is a six-year list of projects that are prioritized and programmed for delivery. The investment strategies for the six-year list of projects are based on performance-based analyses within Programs and Sub-Programs with a ten-year planning horizon. The Plan aligns with WSDOT’s performance-based approach to transportation decision-making.

name: WSDOT - Noise Walls - Proposed
description: Noise walls are free-standing barriers built parallel to a highway. They are usually found near public areas, such as parks, and residential areas. The walls range in height from 6 to 20 feet, but are normally 12 to 15 feet tall. They can reduce noise levels by 10 to 15 decibels, cutting the loudness of traffic noise in half. name: WSDOT - Environmental Mitigation Sites
description: This is a polygon layer showing the spatial extent of WSDOT owned mitigation sites that will be updated annually. Accuracy is limited to best information available, so varies from site to site. Data was gathered from .dgn files, georeferenced plan sheets, and/or aerial photos. This is intended to be as complete of an inventory as possible of all WSDOT owned environmental mitigation sites. Last Updated May 2023.

It is maintained by the WSDOT Office of Emergency Management. Updates to this dataset are on an as needed basis. The sensitive areas are represented by lines segments which have had their beginning and ending points buffered an additional 300 feet. Data has been synchronized to the LRS December, 31 2017.
